llmWorkflowPrompt.purpose: Re-execute the repo's current workflow for producing the Ornn Remotion video whenever the source research documents change. Follow the existing architecture, keep the work deterministic, and make only the minimum necessary edits.
llmWorkflowPrompt.role: You are a coding assistant operating inside this repository. Your job is to read the current source documents, update the existing Remotion implementation under video/, run validation, and leave the repo in a reviewable state.

file.role: selective source for closing asks, gating questions, and management-call framing

coreWorkflow.principle: Do not build or maintain a generic Markdown-to-slides system for this repo. The workflow is manual editorial curation from prose into typed scene data.
Treat video/src/data/ornn-ic-v1.ts as the primary implementation of the current narrative. Update that data first, then update composition/components only if the new content requires it.
Keep the video institutional, restrained, high-signal, and deterministic. This is an investor memo in motion, not a product promo.
Preserve the existing standalone Remotion app under video/. Update in place; do not rebuild the app from scratch.
Keep v1 silent unless the user explicitly asks for audio. Do not add narration, music, or sound design as part of this workflow.

idempotenceRules.rule: Start by inspecting the current repo state, including uncommitted changes. Never revert unrelated user edits.
Compare the latest source documents against the current scene data and update only the scenes, citations, and layout details that are materially affected.
If the current implementation already reflects the source documents closely enough, make no source edits. Report a no-op result rather than churning formatting or copy.
Keep stable file names, scene identifiers, component names, and composition IDs unless a change is genuinely required by the updated narrative.
Prefer editing existing files over creating new abstractions. Add new files or components only when the current structure cannot cleanly support the revised content.
Do not modify the source memo or diligence list unless the user explicitly asks you to rewrite those documents.
Do not change the repo workflow itself unless required. Update video/README.md or video/v1-review-notes.md only if the workflow, scope choices, or review focus have actually changed.

scene.purpose: Establish company, date, recommendation, and gating issues immediately.
Frame the market-structure thesis and why benchmark ownership matters.
Show visible product, partner, and distribution signal.

source.section: Core risk #1: legal and regulatory ambiguity

scene.purpose: Show the largest gating issue around legal structure and regulatory mapping.

source.section: Core risk #2: counterparty, collateral, and balance-sheet risk

scene.purpose: Frame the business as principal-risk / market-infrastructure risk, not pure SaaS.

source.section: Core risk #3: OCPI governance and liquidity proof

scene.purpose: Show benchmark-governance and market-depth proof gaps.
Close on the recommendation and present the highest-priority next-step asks.

executionPlan.step: Read the two source documents, the build plan, the review notes, and the existing files under video/src/. Inspect git status before editing so you do not overwrite unrelated work.

Compare the latest memo and diligence list against the current implementation. Identify which scene claims, dates, figures, questions, and citations changed. Do not assume every scene needs rewriting.
Edit video/src/data/ornn-ic-v1.ts first. Keep the narrative concise and investor-facing. Preserve existing scene IDs where possible so downstream code stays stable.
Edit video/src/data/citations.ts to keep each scene's citation footer aligned with the revised claims and sources.

Update video/src/compositions/OrnnIcV1.tsx, video/src/components/, video/src/theme.ts, and video/src/utils/ only when the revised copy no longer fits, the visual hierarchy needs to change, or the scene structure genuinely changed.
Keep the video text-led and diagram-led. Do not introduce live data visualization, generic slide generation, or unrelated product features. Continue using citations and restrained motion.

Target roughly 105 to 120 seconds at 30 fps. Prefer tightening copy before changing scene durations. Change durations only when the updated narrative cannot fit cleanly, and explain any timing changes.
Run the project checks and render the main composition after edits. If a check fails, fix the issue or report the blocker clearly.

file.expectation: Primary typed scene content. Update first.
Scene-level citation labels. Keep aligned with source claims.
Main composition sequencing and scene selection.
Reusable scene building blocks. Touch only if content/layout requires it.
Visual system. Keep institutional tone and low churn.
Composition registration. Keep stable unless composition contract changes.
Commands and workflow notes. Update only if workflow changes.
Scope and review focus. Update only if those choices change.

qualityBar.item: Recommendation framing must remain clear in the opening and closing.
Claims, dates, figures, and partner references must match the latest source documents.
The three core risks must remain legible and ordered by investment importance.
The closing scene must reflect the most relevant diligence asks for the next management call.
The visual result must feel institutional and reviewable, not promotional.
The final code should stay simple, typed, and easy to revise on the next iteration.

finalResponseContract.item: Summarize the source changes you detected.
List the files you changed and why.
Report whether the run was a true update or an intentional no-op.
Report the validation commands you ran and their outcomes.
Call out any remaining review risks, especially around editorial judgment or timing.
